overview: A Claude Code skill that enables AI developers to analyze Langfuse traces, design evaluations, and iterate on AI applications. Claude acts as an expert guide and sparring partner with real data access via Python SDK.
goals: Enable Claude to find real issues in traces (surface problems user didn't see)
Allow users to iterate faster on prompts (test variations without leaving Claude Code)
Help users understand their AI apps better (Claude explains and teaches Langfuse concepts)
nonGoals: No MCP server for actions - skill hits API directly via Python SDK
No JavaScript/TypeScript code - Python SDK only
No ad-hoc code generation - pre-made scripts provide determinism
successMetrics: MVP test passes: fetch traces and analyze them (read-only core value)
Non-experts can use skill without prior Langfuse knowledge
Three workflows functional: Debug, Evaluate, Experiment

stack.auth: .env file in project root (LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_BASE_URL)

uiNotes: CLI output format: insight-first with evidence (conclusions before data)
Error messages: codes for Claude + diagnosis for humans

importFormat.description: N/A - skill queries Langfuse API directly

rules: Auth via .env with guidance fallback if missing
Diagnose-first error handling: try auto-diagnosis, fall back to step-by-step guidance
Context-aware education: quick inline + offer deep-dive + use analogies
Workflow suggestions are suggestive, not mandatory
Flush() required for short-lived operations (SDK v3 pattern)

executionDirectives._comment: CRITICAL: These directives guide HOW Claude executes this PRD. Read this section FIRST.

mandatoryContextPerStory.description: Claude MUST read these two files BEFORE starting EACH story. This is non-negotiable.
mandatoryContextPerStory.timing: Before beginning work on ANY user story, re-read both files to ensure full context

files.purpose: Contains all user decisions (D1-D20), hard constraints (H1-H9), and the Council debate results with recommended architecture
files.when: EVERY story - decisions and constraints apply throughout

files.purpose: Contains 86 Ideal State Criteria rows organized by category - the source of truth for what 'done' looks like
files.when: EVERY story - check relevant ISC rows for acceptance criteria

mandatoryContextPerStory.enforcement: STOP. Before writing ANY code for a story, confirm you have read both files in this session. These are not optional background reading - they ARE the specification.

optionalResearchDocs.description: Reference these research documents ON DEMAND when you need specific SDK/API details
optionalResearchDocs.usage: Consult when implementing specific functionality - do NOT read all upfront

files.docs/research/langfuse-tracing-observabi: Trace data model, observation types, sessions

algorithmUsage.description: Use THEALGORITHM skill at STANDARD thoroughness for each story. Council debate is NOT needed since ISC was already created via Council.
algorithmUsage.pattern: For each story: Define Current State → Reference ISC for Ideal State → Iterate until criteria met

tddWorkflow.description: Use Test-Driven Development for all implementation stories
pattern: 1. Write failing test that verifies the acceptance criteria
2. Run test to confirm it fails (red)
3. Write minimal code to make test pass (green)

integrationTestingPhase.description: Integration tests run AFTER core implementation is complete
integrationTestingPhase.method: Spawn subagents that use the new skill to actually query Langfuse and validate responses
integrationTestingPhase.requirements: .env must have valid LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_BASE_URL

stories.title: Project setup and linter configuration
stories.description: As a developer, I want the project scaffolded with proper Python tooling so that code quality is enforced from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Skill directory created at .claude/skills/langfuse/ with SKILL.md placeholder
pyproject.toml created with project metadata and dependencies (langfuse, python-dotenv)
ruff.toml created with sensible defaults for Python linting
pytest configured in pyproject.toml
scripts/ directory created
references/ directory created
lib/ directory created
Example: Running 'ruff check .' returns no errors on empty project
Negative case: Missing pyproject.toml -> clear error about project setup

stories.title: Create shared utilities module
stories.description: As a developer, I want shared Langfuse utilities so that all scripts use consistent patterns for auth, client init, and error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/langfuse_utils.py created with LangfuseClient wrapper class
Auth loading from .env (LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_BASE_URL)
Error code constants defined (AUTH_MISSING, AUTH_INVALID, NETWORK_ERROR, RATE_LIMITED)
Human-readable error messages for each code
flush() helper for short-lived operations
Example: Missing .env -> returns AUTH_MISSING code with guidance message
Example: Invalid keys -> returns AUTH_INVALID with 'Check your API keys' message
Negative case: Empty LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY -> AUTH_MISSING, not silent failure
ISC reference: rows 53-59

stories.title: Create SKILL.md routing table
stories.description: As Claude, I want a SKILL.md file that routes user intents to appropriate scripts and references so that the skill activates correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md under 200 lines (routing table only)
Description under 1024 chars with trigger keywords: langfuse, traces, spans, generations, evals, scores, datasets, experiments
Routes to scripts/langfuse.py for actions
Routes to references/ for deep-dive documentation
Progressive loading architecture (SKILL.md as router)
Example: User says 'analyze my langfuse traces' -> skill activates
Negative case: User says 'analyze my logs' without langfuse context -> skill does NOT activate
ISC reference: rows 1-6

stories.title: Create single entry point with subcommand routing
stories.description: As Claude, I want a single Python entry point with subcommands so that I can deterministically execute Langfuse operations.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/langfuse.py created as single entry point
Subcommands implemented: trace, evaluate, experiment, setup
Uses argparse or click for CLI parsing
Imports from lib/langfuse_utils.py for shared functionality
Auth validation before any operation (calls auth_check from utils)
Example: 'python scripts/langfuse.py setup check' -> validates connection
Example: 'python scripts/langfuse.py trace list' -> shows help for trace subcommand
Negative case: 'python scripts/langfuse.py unknown' -> clear error about valid subcommands
ISC reference: rows 7-13

stories.title: Implement setup subcommand
stories.description: As a user, I want setup commands so that I can verify my Langfuse connection and diagnose auth issues.
acceptanceCriteria: 'setup check' verifies auth and connection to Langfuse
'setup diagnose' detects common issues: wrong region (EU vs US), expired keys, invalid keys
'setup guide' walks through setup step-by-step
Clear next steps provided on any failure
Example: Wrong region -> 'Your keys appear to be for EU cloud, but LANGFUSE_BASE_URL points to US'
Example: Valid setup -> 'Connected to Langfuse at [url]. Project: [name]'
Negative case: Network timeout -> retry 3x with backoff, then clear error
ISC reference: rows 35-40

stories.title: Implement trace list command
stories.description: As a user, I want to list recent traces so that I can see what's happening in my AI application.
acceptanceCriteria: 'trace list' fetches recent traces with optional filters
Supports filters: --limit, --name, --user-id, --session-id
Uses v2 Observations API with cursor-based pagination
Output shows: trace ID, name, timestamp, status (success/error)
Progress indicator for fetches >5 seconds
Example: 'trace list --limit 10' -> shows 10 most recent traces
Example: 'trace list --name chatbot' -> filters by trace name
Negative case: No traces found -> 'No traces found matching your criteria'
ISC reference: rows 14, 19

stories.title: Implement trace get command
stories.description: As a user, I want to get details of a specific trace so that I can understand what happened in that execution.
acceptanceCriteria: 'trace get <id>' fetches single trace with all observations
Output shows hierarchy: Session -> Trace -> Observations
Displays observation types (generation, span, event, etc.)
Shows timing, input/output, model, cost for each observation
Example: Valid trace ID -> full trace tree with observations
Negative case: Invalid trace ID -> 'Trace not found: [id]'
ISC reference: rows 15, 20

stories.title: Implement trace analyze command
stories.description: As a user, I want to analyze a trace for issues so that Claude can help me find bottlenecks and problems.
acceptanceCriteria: 'trace analyze <id>' analyzes latency and finds bottlenecks
Output is insight-first: key findings before supporting data
Identifies slowest observations and their contribution to total time
Calculates p50, p95, p99 if analyzing multiple traces
Example: 'Your p95 latency is 3.2s, caused by the embedding-lookup span (2.8s average)'
Example: Trace with errors -> highlights error observations first
Negative case: Trace has no timing data -> 'Cannot analyze: no timing data available'
ISC reference: rows 16, 21, 69

stories.title: Implement trace errors and costs commands
stories.description: As a user, I want specialized commands for finding errors and understanding costs.
acceptanceCriteria: 'trace errors' finds traces with errors in observations
'trace costs' shows cost breakdown by model, trace, or time period
Errors command shows: trace ID, error message, timestamp, observation that failed
Costs command shows: total cost, cost per model, cost per trace (top N)
Example: 'trace errors --since 24h' -> errors in last 24 hours
Example: 'trace costs --group-by model' -> cost breakdown by model
Negative case: No errors found -> 'No errors in the specified time range'
ISC reference: rows 17, 18

stories.title: Implement evaluate subcommand
stories.description: As a user, I want evaluation commands so that I can score traces and design evaluation strategies.
acceptanceCriteria: 'evaluate design' helps design evaluation strategy interactively
'evaluate score <trace_id>' creates a score on a trace
'evaluate scores' lists scores for the project
Supports all score types: NUMERIC, CATEGORICAL, BOOLEAN
Score command accepts: --name, --value, --comment, --data-type
Example: 'evaluate score abc123 --name quality --value 0.8 --data-type numeric'
Example: 'evaluate scores --trace abc123' -> all scores for that trace
Negative case: Invalid score type -> 'Invalid data-type. Use: numeric, categorical, boolean'
ISC reference: rows 22-25

stories.title: Implement experiment subcommand
stories.description: As a user, I want experiment commands so that I can create datasets and run A/B tests on my prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: 'experiment create-dataset' creates a new dataset
'experiment add-item' adds an item to a dataset
'experiment run' runs experiment using run_experiment() API
'experiment compare' compares results of experiment runs
Uses concurrent execution (run_experiment() handles this)
Example: 'experiment create-dataset --name test-cases --description "Golden test cases"'
Example: 'experiment run --dataset test-cases --model gpt-4o'
Negative case: Dataset not found -> 'Dataset "[name]" not found. Use experiment create-dataset first.'
ISC reference: rows 29-34

stories.title: Create getting-started reference
stories.description: As a new user, I want a getting-started guide so that I can use the skill without prior Langfuse knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: references/getting-started.md created
Readable in <2 minutes
Covers: auth setup, first trace query, three workflows (debug, evaluate, experiment)
Uses analogies for non-experts: 'Think of traces like detailed logs for each AI request'
Links to deep-dive references for more detail
Example: New user follows guide -> successfully runs 'trace list'
Negative case: N/A (documentation)
ISC reference: rows 41-43, 66

stories.title: Create domain reference files
stories.description: As Claude, I want domain reference files so that I can provide deep-dive explanations when users need them.
acceptanceCriteria: references/traces.md created with trace domain deep-dive
references/evals.md created with evaluation deep-dive
references/datasets.md created with dataset/experiment deep-dive
Each file has TOC (expected >100 lines)
traces.md covers: data model, observation types, cost tracking
evals.md covers: score types, LLM-as-Judge, annotation queues
datasets.md covers: run_experiment(), golden datasets, linking traces to items
Example: Claude reads evals.md -> can explain LLM-as-Judge with analogies
Negative case: N/A (documentation)
ISC reference: rows 44-52

stories.title: Add context-aware education to commands
stories.description: As a non-expert user, I want Claude to explain concepts inline so that I learn while using the skill.
acceptanceCriteria: Commands detect when concepts might be unfamiliar
Quick inline explanation + offer deep-dive option
Uses analogies when helpful: 'Scores are like unit tests for AI responses'
Explains LLM-as-Judge when running evaluation commands
Explains golden datasets when creating datasets
Example: User runs first evaluate command -> brief explanation of scoring concepts
Example: User asks 'what is LLM-as-Judge?' -> detailed explanation with examples
Negative case: Expert user -> can skip explanations with --quiet flag
ISC reference: rows 26-28, 33, 67

stories.title: MVP validation and integration testing
stories.description: As a developer, I want to validate the MVP works end-to-end with real Langfuse data.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests created in tests/integration/
Tests use .env credentials to connect to real Langfuse
MVP test passes: fetch traces and analyze them
Setup check passes with valid credentials
Trace list returns real traces from Langfuse
Trace analyze provides meaningful insights on real data
Example: Run integration tests -> all pass with real Langfuse connection
Negative case: Missing .env -> integration tests skip with clear message
ISC reference: rows 72-77

stories.title: Final validation against ISC
stories.description: As a developer, I want to verify all 86 ISC criteria are met before declaring the skill complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Review all 86 ISC rows from docs/langfuse-skill-ISC.md
Mark each row as DONE or document why not applicable
All hard constraints (H1-H9) verified
All success criteria (D9) validated
Manual walkthrough of three workflows: debug, evaluate, experiment
Example: ISC row 1 'SKILL.md exists at langfuse/SKILL.md' -> verified DONE
Negative case: Any ISC row fails -> document blocker and fix before completion
ISC reference: ALL rows (1-86)
